content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and SSS.

Find any array A=[A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã] of length NNN such that:


Each AiA_iAi‚Äã is a **positive** integer.
The sum of the array is SSS, i.e, A1+A2+‚Ä¶+AN=SA_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_N = SA1‚Äã+A2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+AN‚Äã=S.
The bitwise XOR of the array is 000, i.e, A1‚äïA2‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïAN=0A_1\oplus A_2\oplus\ldots\oplus A_N = 0A1‚Äã‚äïA2‚Äã‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïAN‚Äã=0.
If multiple valid arrays exist, you can find any of them.

If no valid array exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and SSS ‚Äî the length of the array and the desired sum.


Output Format
For each test case:



If no valid array exists, output a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise output NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array you found.

If multiple possible valid arrays exist, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the array A=[7,1,10,12]A = [7, 1, 10, 12]A=[7,1,10,12]. The sum of this array is 7+1+10+12=307+1+10+12 = 307+1+10+12=30, and its bitwise XOR is 7‚äï1‚äï10‚äï12=07\oplus 1\oplus 10\oplus 12 = 07‚äï1‚äï10‚äï12=0, as required.
Test case 222: Consider the array A=[5,3,6]A = [5, 3, 6]A=[5,3,6]. The sum of this array is 5+3+6=145+3+6=145+3+6=14, and its bitwise XOR is 5‚äï3‚äï6=05\oplus 3\oplus 6 = 05‚äï3‚äï6=0, as required.
Test case 333: No valid array of length 222, sum 555, and XOR 000 exists.
